title: V-ing / p.p. 作形容詞修飾名詞

formulas.zh: 現在分詞修飾名詞，表示名詞正在進行的動作或主動性質
過去分詞修飾名詞，表示名詞所承受的動作或完成的狀態

explanation: 分詞作形容詞修飾名詞時，單個分詞通常前置於名詞，分詞片語則後置修飾。V-ing表主動或進行中的動作（如 a running boy 正在跑步的男孩），p.p.表被動或已完成的動作（如 a broken window 破損的窗戶）。此句型常見於學測閱讀與寫作，須注意分詞的邏輯主語與名詞的一致性，區分前置單詞與後置片語的位置差異。

examples.eng: The sleeping child looked peaceful and innocent in the warm sunlight.
examples.chi: 那個正在睡覺的孩子在溫暖陽光下看起來平靜而無辜。

examples.eng: The abandoned dog found in the shelter was adopted by a kind family yesterday.
examples.chi: 在收容所發現的被遺棄狗狗昨天被一個善心家庭領養了。

examples.eng: Students studying for the exam realized that time management was crucial for success.
examples.chi: 正在為考試準備的學生們意識到時間管理對成功至關重要。

examples.eng: The damaged buildings caused by the earthquake were carefully reconstructed over five years.
examples.chi: 地震造成的受損建築物在五年內被仔細重建。

extension.tip: 區分主被動：V-ing表名詞主動執行動作（a reading student 正在讀書的學生），p.p.表名詞被動接受動作（a read book 被讀過的書）。判斷時問「名詞是否主動做這件事」，若是用V-ing，若被動接受則用p.p.。注意片語長度決定位置：簡短用前置，複雜用後置。

examples.test: a crying baby vs. a cried baby
examples.result: → crying baby正確（嬰兒在哭）；cried baby不自然，p.p.用於如a beaten baby（被毆打的嬰兒）等被動狀態

examples.test: a walking man vs. a walked path
examples.result: → walking man正確（男人正在走路）；walked path不常用，應用walked-on path或well-trodden path表示被踩過的小徑

examples.test: students studying hard vs. problems solved carefully
examples.result: → 前者V-ing片語後置（學生正努力讀書），後者p.p.片語後置（問題被仔細解決），兩者邏輯主語與名詞一致

extension.note: 記住：V-ing=主動進行中，p.p.=被動已完成，分詞位置看複雜度，短詞前置長片語後置。

parts: girl waved goodbye to her family at the airport.
exercises.sentence: The departing girl waved goodbye to her family at the airport.
exercises.translation: 那個正在離開的女孩在機場向她的家人揮手告別。

parts: during the archaeological dig was carefully preserved in the museum.
exercises.sentence: The ancient vase discovered during the archaeological dig was carefully preserved in the museum.
exercises.translation: 在考古挖掘中被發現的古代花瓶在博物館裡被小心保護。

parts: children played happily in the park all afternoon.
exercises.sentence: The laughing children played happily in the park all afternoon.
exercises.translation: 那些正在笑的孩子們整個下午都在公園裡開心地玩耍。

parts: in the fire were considered lost forever until someone recovered them.
exercises.sentence: The documents burned in the fire were considered lost forever until someone recovered them.
exercises.translation: 在火災中被燒毀的文件被認為永久丟失了，直到有人發現它們。

vocab_table.zh: 令人興奮的、激動人心的

mc_exercises.question_chi: 選擇適當的分詞形式修飾名詞，完成句子。
mc_exercises.question_eng: The _______ students finished their homework and went home happily.
mc_exercises.explanation: 正確答案是 B (studying)。句子表達「正在學習的學生」，需用 V-ing 形式表主動/進行中的動作。A 項 studied（過去分詞）表被動，不符合邏輯；C 項 being studied 過於複雜且不符合修飾用法；D 項 study 非分詞形式。

mc_exercises.question_chi: 選擇正確的分詞形式，修飾後面的名詞。
mc_exercises.question_eng: The _______ window in the old building needs to be repaired immediately.
mc_exercises.explanation: 正確答案是 C (broken)。過去分詞 broken 表被動和已完成的狀態，表示「已破損的窗戶」。A 項 breaking 表主動進行，邏輯不符；B 項 broke 是過去式動詞，不能作形容詞；D 項 being broken 過於冗長。

mc_exercises.question_chi: 區分單個分詞與分詞片語的位置差異，選擇正確選項。
mc_exercises.question_eng: The _______ dog that we found on the street yesterday was very weak and thin.
mc_exercises.explanation: 正確答案是 A (abandoned)。單個過去分詞應前置於名詞，表「被遺棄的狗」。C 項是分詞片語但應後置（在關代詞之後），在此位置不適；B 項 abandoning 為主動，邏輯不符；D 項文法錯誤（abandon 用主動應為 abandoned by owner）。

mc_exercises.question_chi: 綜合考察邏輯主語、分詞位置與片語使用，選擇最適選項。
mc_exercises.question_eng: The _______ documents found in the sealed envelope were revealed to contain confidential information about the missing operation.

options.text: yellowed and aged discovered in the sealed envelope

mc_exercises.explanation: 正確答案是 A (yellowed and aging)。兩個單個分詞前置修飾名詞，yellowed（已變黃）+ aging（逐漸老化）分別表完成與進行狀態。B 項 yellowing 與 aged 搭配不協調；C 項包含 discovered 重複後面 found 的意思；D 項片語應單獨後置，不應與前置分詞混用。

translation_exercises.sentence_chi: 昨天我看到一個正在哭泣的小女孩坐在公園的長椅上。
translation_exercises.answer: Yesterday I saw a crying girl sitting on the bench in the park.
key_points: sitting on the bench（分詞片語後置修飾）
hints: 提示1：單個分詞 crying 前置修飾 girl；分詞片語 sitting on... 後置修飾 girl
提示2：注意 cry 的 V-ing 形式是 crying（雙寫 y 不變），sitting 才是雙寫 t

translation_exercises.sentence_chi: 這些被損壞的教科書需要送去修理。
translation_exercises.answer: These damaged textbooks need to be repaired.
key_points: need to be repaired（謂語動詞）
hints: 提示1：p.p. damaged 前置修飾名詞 textbooks，表示教科書已經被損壞
提示2：不要混淆『被損壞的教科書』(damaged textbooks) 與『正在損壞教科書的人』(damaging textbooks)；damaged 是完成式被動，符合邏輯

translation_exercises.sentence_chi: 那位努力準備考試的學生最終考上了他夢想的大學。
translation_exercises.answer: The student preparing for the exam finally got admitted to his dream university. / The student preparing for the exam finally got into the university of his dreams.
key_points: preparing for the exam（V-ing分詞片語後置修飾 student）
dream university 或 university of his dreams
hints: 提示1：『準備考試的學生』用 the student preparing for the exam，V-ing 片語後置；若寫成 the preparing student 會顯得不自然
提示2：注意 dream 作形容詞（dream university）與名詞（university of his dreams）的差異；此句型應使用 V-ing 片語而非 who is preparing

translation_exercises.sentence_chi: 當地居民發現了一個被遺棄的嬰兒，他們立即聯絡了相關部門來幫助這個可憐的孩子。
translation_exercises.answer: Local residents discovered an abandoned baby, and they immediately contacted the relevant department to help the unfortunate child.
key_points: an abandoned baby（單詞前置修飾）
邏輯主語與被修飾名詞的一致
hints: 提示1：『被遺棄的嬰兒』用 abandoned baby，p.p. 表示已經被遺棄（被動完成）；不能用 abandoning baby（會變成『正在遺棄嬰兒』的錯誤意思）
提示2：『可憐的孩子』可用 unfortunate child 或 pitiful child；注意 abandoned 必須前置（單詞），若要後置需變成分詞片語如 the baby abandoned by parents，但此句用前置更自然簡潔
